data.narrative:
TS Conductor, a Series B climate and energy company founded by Jason Huang, is evaluated against a comparable universe of 9 peer companies—one public (LNZA) and eight private frontier-tech operators in the same sector and stage. The peer set includes Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), and CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), among others, with valuations ranging from $120M to $5.0B.

Two valuation methods are applicable. The stage-progression approach uses the company's $60M total raised as a proxy valuation and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage step-ups, implying $114M. The peer-median method across six same-sector, same-stage comparables yields $355M, the central tendency of a cohort with a $235M spread between lower and upper quartiles.

Triangulating these two methods produces a median valuation of $234M, with a low bound of $97M and high bound of $408M. The wide range reflects material heterogeneity within the climate-tech peer set, driven primarily by differences in capital intensity, commercialization progress, and technology risk. Key uncertainties include the appropriate positioning within the peer distribution—whether TS Conductor's technology, burn rate, and customer traction align more closely with the $120M−$310M cluster or the outlier valuations above.
